**Description.** A cluster of lichen-crusted standing stones marks an old boundary of the deep wood. Their faces bear worn glyphs and arrows, half-erased by centuries of frost — a key, if anyone can read it, to which of the diverging trails ahead is safe.

**Resolution.** Deciphering the markers takes a **DC 13 Intelligence (History or Investigation)** check, or a **DC 13 Wisdom (Survival)** read of the old trail-signs. Success reveals which path avoids the worse country ahead (granting advantage on the next travel check or skipping the next hazard). Failure isn't dangerous in itself, but a guessed path may lead the party astray and cost a day. Comprehending the glyphs magically solves it outright.

environments.name: Whining Midge Bog
environments.check: Constitution saving throw (Survival/Medicine to ward off the swarms)
environments.summary: Clouds of biting insects that torment travelers and fray their nerves.
environments.body:
Whining Midge Bog
=================

*Environmental challenge — low severity*

**Description.** The air over this stagnant bog hums with a maddening whine. Dense clouds of midges and biting flies rise from the reeds, crawling into eyes, ears, and every gap in clothing.

**Resolution.** A creature that crosses without protection makes a **DC 12 Constitution saving throw**, taking 2 (1d4) poison damage and having disadvantage on its next travel check from the relentless biting; on a success it shrugs off the worst. A **DC 12 Wisdom (Survival or Medicine)** check — using smoke, mud, or pungent herbs — wards off the swarms for the crossing. Fire, wind, or a brisk pace also disperses them.

environments.name: Bramble-Choked Ravine
environments.check: Strength (Athletics) to climb/cut through; Dexterity to avoid the worst thorns
environments.summary: A steep gully clogged with iron-hard thorns that tear at any who descend.
environments.body:
Bramble-Choked Ravine
=====================

*Environmental challenge — moderate severity*

**Description.** The trail drops into a narrow ravine that decades of neglect have filled with a dense, woody tangle of thorns — some as long as a finger, hard as iron, and hooked to hold what they catch. The far side beckons, but the only way is through.

**Resolution.** Forcing a path down and across requires a **DC 14 Strength (Athletics)** check to climb and cut through. On a failure, the thorns rake the creature for 5 (2d4) slashing damage and snag its gear, slowing the whole party. Hacking a corridor with blades, burning a path, or a **DC 14 Dexterity (Acrobatics)** pick through the gaps reduces the damage or grants advantage. Heavily armoured creatures have an easier climb but still risk the hooks.

environments.name: Crumbling Rope Bridge
environments.check: Dexterity (Acrobatics) to cross; Dexterity save when it gives way
environments.summary: A rotted span over a deep ravine, one frayed rope from collapse.
environments.body:
Crumbling Rope Bridge
=====================

*Environmental challenge — moderate severity*

**Description.** An old rope-and-plank bridge sags across a deep ravine. Half its slats are missing, the ropes are frayed and grey, and it groans with every gust of wind.

**Resolution.** Crossing calls for a **DC 15 Dexterity (Acrobatics)** check; moving slowly and testing each plank grants advantage but costs time. On a failure, a plank or rope gives way and the creature must succeed on a **DC 15 Dexterity saving throw** to catch the bridge (ending up dangling), or fall 17 (5d6) bludgeoning damage to the ravine floor. Heavy loads or multiple creatures at once may collapse the span outright.

environments.name: Deep Crevasse
environments.check: Strength (Athletics) to climb; Survival/Perception to find a route
environments.summary: A yawning split in the rock or ice that bars the direct path.
environments.body:
Deep Crevasse
=============

*Environmental challenge — moderate severity*

**Description.** The ground splits into a deep, shadowed crevasse — too wide to leap and too sheer to descend casually. Cold air rises from the dark below.

**Resolution.** The party can search for a natural span or narrow point (**DC 14 Wisdom (Survival)** or Perception), climb down and across (**DC 14 Strength (Athletics)**, fall damage by depth on a failure), or bridge it with rope and gear. A roped climber who fails is caught by the line rather than falling. Skipping the search and free-climbing risks the full drop.

environments.name: Fast-Flowing River Crossing
environments.check: Strength (Athletics) or Wisdom (Survival) to find a ford
environments.summary: A cold, fast river barring the way, its current deceptively strong.
environments.body:
Fast-Flowing River Crossing
===========================

*Environmental challenge — moderate severity*

**Description.** A broad mountain river cuts across the route, its surface deceptively smooth over a powerful current. The far bank is close, but the footing beneath is slick and the water bone-cold.

**Resolution.** Crossing requires a **DC 14 Strength (Athletics)** check; a **Wisdom (Survival)** check can first locate a shallower ford to grant advantage. On a failure, the creature is swept downstream, taking 7 (2d6) bludgeoning damage against rocks and arriving soaked (doused torches, ruined rations). A rope line or aid from an ally grants advantage.

environments.name: Freezing Sleet Storm
environments.check: Constitution saving throw (Survival to shelter or press on safely)
environments.summary: Driving icy rain that glazes everything and chills travelers to the bone.
environments.body:
Freezing Sleet Storm
====================

*Environmental challenge — moderate severity*

**Description.** The sky opens with a stinging, half-frozen rain that soaks through clothing in minutes and coats every surface in slick, clear ice. Visibility drops, the footing turns glassy, and the cold bites deeper with every hour.

**Resolution.** Pressing on through the storm forces each creature to make a **DC 14 Constitution saving throw**, taking 3 (1d6) cold damage and having disadvantage on Dexterity checks (icy footing) until they warm up; a success halves the cold. A **DC 14 Wisdom (Survival)** check rigs shelter, keeps a fire lit, or finds a lee that lets the party wait out or safely travel the storm with advantage. Cold resistance or magical warmth negates it; a failed party that doesn't dry off risks exhaustion.

environments.name: Quicksand Field
environments.check: Strength (Athletics) to escape; Survival to spot in advance
environments.summary: A deceptive bog that swallows the careless and drags them under.
environments.body:
Quicksand Field
===============

*Environmental challenge — moderate severity*

**Description.** A stretch of boggy ground looks no different from the surrounding mire — until a foot plunges through and the earth itself begins to pull downward.

**Resolution.** A **DC 13 Wisdom (Survival)** check spots the hazard before entering. A creature that steps in sinks; at the start of each of its turns it descends, and a submerged creature begins to suffocate. Escaping requires a **DC 13 Strength (Athletics)** check (made with disadvantage once submerged past the waist), or being hauled out by allies with a rope or pole. Thrashing without a plan only sinks it faster.

environments.name: Snowdrift Field
environments.check: Constitution saving throw (Survival to break trail efficiently)
environments.summary: Thigh-deep snow that saps strength from anyone who tries to cross it.
environments.body:
Snowdrift Field
===============

*Environmental challenge — moderate severity*

**Description.** An open clearing has filled with wind-packed snow, drifted thigh-deep and crusted just hard enough to break through with every step. What looks like a short walk becomes an exhausting wade.

**Resolution.** Crossing the field is difficult terrain that costs hours of hard labour. Each creature makes a **DC 14 Constitution saving throw** at the end of the crossing or gains one level of exhaustion. A **DC 14 Wisdom (Survival)** check to break trail in shifts, fashion snowshoes, or pick the firmest line grants the party advantage on the saves. Flight, a *pass without trace*–style aid, or simply going around (at the cost of more time) avoids it.

environments.name: Thin Ice Lake Crossing
environments.check: Wisdom (Survival) to test the ice; Strength (Athletics) to escape the water
environments.summary: A frozen lake whose ice can give way into killing-cold water beneath.
environments.body:
Thin Ice Lake Crossing
======================

*Environmental challenge — moderate severity*

**Description.** A broad lake offers a tempting shortcut across its frozen surface. But the ice groans and creaks underfoot, thinning treacherously over deep, black, killing-cold water — especially near the inlets and the centre.

**Resolution.** A **DC 14 Wisdom (Survival)** check picks the route over the thickest ice; failure (or a heavy load, or bad luck) means the ice cracks. A creature on breaking ice makes a **DC 14 Dexterity saving throw** to throw itself clear, or plunges in: 7 (2d6) cold damage, and it must spend its turns making **DC 14 Strength (Athletics)** checks to haul out onto solid ice while the cold saps its strength. Spreading the party's weight, crawling, or roping together grants advantage; a soaked creature risks exhaustion if not warmed quickly.

environments.name: Avalanche
environments.check: Dexterity saving throw (then Athletics DC 15 to dig free)
environments.summary: A roaring wall of snow and rock that buries everything in its path.
environments.body:
Avalanche
=========

*Environmental challenge — high severity*

**Description.** A shelf of snow and loose scree gives way high on the slope, and a thundering wall of white and stone roars down toward the party. There are seconds to react before it hits.

**Resolution.** Each creature in the path makes a **DC 17 Dexterity saving throw**. On a failure: 28 (8d6) bludgeoning damage, knocked prone, and **buried** (restrained and suffocating). On a success: half damage and not buried. A buried creature, or an ally reaching it, can free it with a **DC 15 Strength (Athletics)** check. Reaching the edge of the slide before it arrives (a successful save plus the right positioning) avoids it entirely.

environments.name: Collapsing Ice Cavern
environments.check: Dexterity saving throw (Survival to judge the ice, Athletics to dig free)
environments.summary: A glittering ice cave whose ceiling sheds killing slabs without warning.
environments.body:
Collapsing Ice Cavern
=====================

*Environmental challenge — high severity*

**Description.** A glacial cave offers shelter or a shortcut beneath the ice — a breathtaking vault of blue-white walls and frozen pillars. But the ceiling weeps and cracks, and somewhere deep in the ice a deep, grinding groan warns that the whole vault is shifting.

**Resolution.** A **DC 16 Wisdom (Survival or Nature)** check judges the cavern's stability and the safest, quickest route. Loud noise, fire, or a failed read triggers a collapse: each creature beneath it makes a **DC 16 Dexterity saving throw**, taking 17 (5d6) bludgeoning damage and being **buried** (restrained, suffocating) on a failure, or half damage and clear on a success. A buried creature, or an ally reaching it, digs free with a **DC 16 Strength (Athletics)** check. Moving quietly and quickly, or shoring the ice, lowers the chance of a fall.

environments.name: Glacial Flash Flood
environments.check: Strength (Athletics) or Constitution; risk of drowning
environments.summary: A meltwater torrent that surges down a ravine without warning.
environments.body:
Glacial Flash Flood
===================

*Environmental challenge — high severity*

**Description.** A distant crack echoes as an ice dam fails upstream. Moments later a churning wall of frigid meltwater and debris surges down the ravine, filling it shoulder-deep in seconds.

**Resolution.** Each creature caught in the channel makes a **DC 16 Strength (Athletics)** check (or Constitution save against the cold shock). On a failure: 14 (4d6) bludgeoning damage and swept downstream, then begin to drown unless they surface (a creature can hold its breath, but must reach the bank). On a success: the creature holds footing or grabs an anchor. Scrambling to high ground before the surge arrives avoids it; soaked gear and doused torches are likely either way.

environments.name: Rockslide
environments.check: Dexterity saving throw (Athletics/Acrobatics to scramble clear)
environments.summary: A sudden cascade of stone down a steep pass.
environments.body:
Rockslide
=========

*Environmental challenge — high severity*

**Description.** A narrow pass gives a deceptive footing of loose stone. A misstep — or a deliberate shove from above — sends tonnes of rock tumbling down onto the trail.

**Resolution.** Each creature in the slide's path makes a **DC 16 Dexterity saving throw**, taking 14 (4d6) bludgeoning damage and being knocked prone on a failure, or half damage on a success. The fallen rock leaves the pass as difficult terrain (or blocks it entirely), forcing a detour or a climb.

environments.name: Scalding Geyser Field
environments.check: Wisdom (Survival) to read the vents; Dexterity saving throw to dodge an eruption
environments.summary: A field of hot springs and geysers that erupt in bursts of scalding steam.
environments.body:
Scalding Geyser Field
=====================

*Environmental challenge — high severity*

**Description.** Steam hisses from cracks in the rock, and pools of mineral-bright water bubble at a rolling boil. The ground is a crust of brittle stone over superheated water, and every so often a geyser blasts skyward without warning, wreathing the field in scalding mist.

**Resolution.** Picking a safe line takes a **DC 16 Wisdom (Survival or Nature)** check to read the steam, mineral stains, and timing of the vents. A creature caught when a geyser erupts — or one that fails the read — must make a **DC 16 Dexterity saving throw**, taking 14 (4d6) fire damage on a failure (half on a success) from the scalding water and steam. Thin crust can also give way; a careful, roped crossing or fire resistance greatly reduces the danger.

environments.name: Whiteout Blizzard
environments.check: Constitution saving throw (Survival to navigate / shelter, or be lost)
environments.summary: A howling blizzard that blinds, freezes, and scatters those caught in it.
environments.body:
Whiteout Blizzard
=================

*Environmental challenge — high severity*

**Description.** Without warning the wind rises to a scream and the world vanishes into a roaring wall of white. An arm's length of visibility, cold that knifes through any cloak, and a wind that steals breath and direction alike — this is the storm that kills the unprepared.

**Resolution.** Each creature makes a **DC 16 Constitution saving throw** for each hour in the open, taking 10 (3d6) cold damage and one level of exhaustion on a failure, or half damage and no exhaustion on a success. Worse, the party is **blinded and lost**: a **DC 16 Wisdom (Survival)** check is required to hold course or find shelter, and failure means scattering, losing a day, and risking separation. Digging in, roping the party together, or magical warmth and guidance can turn a deadly storm into a merely miserable one.

nothing.name: Nothing Found
nothing.summary: A quiet travel beat with no treasure — just flavour and atmosphere.

sections: A quiet, uneventful day of travel under an open sky.
Animal tracks crisscross the trail, leading nowhere in particular.
The cold, scattered ashes of an old campsite, long abandoned.
Strange but harmless sounds drift through the trees and slowly fade.
A ruined structure, roofless and long since picked clean by scavengers.
A breathtaking view opens up across the wooded valley below.
A doe and her fawn watch warily from the treeline before bolting.
Birdsong and an easy, pleasant trail make for good, untroubled miles.
A cold spring bubbles up from the rocks, its water clean and sweet.
Woodsmoke drifts on the wind from somewhere distant and unseen.
A field of wildflowers blooms improbably out of season.
A weathered cairn marks a forgotten grave beside the path.

nothing.body:
Nothing Found
=============

The party finds no treasure on this beat. Roll **d12** (or run `tools/loot.py`)
for a bit of flavour. These are atmosphere, not items, so they carry no source link.

1. A quiet, uneventful day of travel under an open sky.
2. Animal tracks crisscross the trail, leading nowhere in particular.
3. The cold, scattered ashes of an old campsite, long abandoned.
4. Strange but harmless sounds drift through the trees and slowly fade.
5. A ruined structure, roofless and long since picked clean by scavengers.
6. A breathtaking view opens up across the wooded valley below.
7. A doe and her fawn watch warily from the treeline before bolting.
8. Birdsong and an easy, pleasant trail make for good, untroubled miles.
9. A cold spring bubbles up from the rocks, its water clean and sweet.
10. Woodsmoke drifts on the wind from somewhere distant and unseen.
11. A field of wildflowers blooms improbably out of season.
12. A weathered cairn marks a forgotten grave beside the path.

mundane.name: Mundane Treasure
mundane.summary: Non-magical treasure and valuable objects — coins, goods, and curiosities.

sections: **Abandoned coin cache** — 4d10 gp spilled from a rotted pouch under a stone.
**Fine silver goblet** — A tarnished but well-worked drinking cup (25 gp).
**Ornate hunting horn** — A carved horn that still sounds a clear, ringing note (50 gp).
**Pouch of cut gemstones** — A handful of small polished stones (50 gp).
[**Masterwork weapon**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=weapon&f=equipment) — A flawlessly balanced blade or haft; no bonus, but worth several times the normal price.
[**Masterwork artisan's tools**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=artisan%27s+tools&f=equipment) — A pristine set of a single trade's tools, prized by any craftsman.
**Bolt of fine silk** — Yards of shimmering cloth, light and astonishingly valuable.
**Ivory figurine** — A small carved animal or idol of warm, yellowed ivory.
**Bundle of rare spices** — Aromatic spices sealed in waxed cloth, worth 75 gp to the right buyer.
**Map to a nearby point of interest** — A hand-drawn sketch marking a ruin, cache, or lair a short way off.
**Valuable monster parts** — Hide, scale, venom, or horn that an alchemist or smith will pay well for.
**Locked strongbox** — A small iron coffer holding 2d10 x 10 gp, if you can open it.
**Old purse of mixed coin** — A worn leather purse holding 3d6 x 5 gp in assorted coins.
**Silver-inlaid bone dice** — A gambler's set of dice, beautifully made (15 gp).
[**Healer's kit and antitoxin**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=healer%27s+kit&f=equipment) — A full healer's kit plus a vial of antitoxin (advantage vs. poison).
**Two flasks of dwarven spirits** — Strong, peat-dark liquor in stoppered flasks (40 gp the pair).
**Fur-lined traveler's cloak** — A heavy, weatherproof cloak well suited to the cold (15 gp).
**Carved jade pendant** — A pale green pendant on a leather cord (100 gp).
[**A week of trail rations and supplies**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=rations&f=equipment) — Preserved food and sundries enough to keep a traveler a week.
[**Signet ring of a minor noble house**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=signet+ring&f=equipment) — A silver seal-ring bearing an unfamiliar crest (25 gp).
[**Tattered spellbook**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=spellbook&f=equipment) — A water-damaged book with one still-legible 1st-level spell.
[**Climber's kit and silk rope**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=climber%27s+kit&f=equipment) — A full climber's kit coiled with 50 ft of strong silk rope.
**Tanned hide of a rare beast** — A supple, unusual pelt sought by trappers and tailors (60 gp).
[**Quiver of masterwork arrows**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=arrows&f=equipment) — Twenty perfectly fletched arrows that fly true.

mundane.body:
Mundane Treasure
================

Mundane treasure and valuable objects. Roll on the table below (d24, or run
`tools/loot.py`).

Gold values are tuned for a baseline party of four at low level. `tools/loot.py`
(and the web app) **auto-scale** every coin and value amount by party size and
average level, so the numbers below are the un-scaled baseline.

1. **Abandoned coin cache** — 4d10 gp spilled from a rotted pouch under a stone.
2. **Fine silver goblet** — A tarnished but well-worked drinking cup (25 gp).
3. **Ornate hunting horn** — A carved horn that still sounds a clear, ringing note (50 gp).
4. **Pouch of cut gemstones** — A handful of small polished stones (50 gp).

11. **Valuable monster parts** — Hide, scale, venom, or horn that an alchemist or smith will pay well for.

15. [**Healer's kit and antitoxin**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=healer%27s+kit&f=equipment) — A full healer's kit plus a vial of antitoxin (advantage vs. poison).
16. **Two flasks of dwarven spirits** — Strong, peat-dark liquor in stoppered flasks (40 gp the pair).
17. **Fur-lined traveler's cloak** — A heavy, weatherproof cloak well suited to the cold (15 gp).
18. **Carved jade pendant** — A pale green pendant on a leather cord (100 gp).
19. [**A week of trail rations and supplies**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=rations&f=equipment) — Preserved food and sundries enough to keep a traveler a week.
20. [**Signet ring of a minor noble house**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=signet+ring&f=equipment) — A silver seal-ring bearing an unfamiliar crest (25 gp).

magic.name: Magic Treasure
magic.summary: Magic items grouped by rarity. Higher party level skews toward rarer tiers.

Common: [**Potion of Healing**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=potion+of+healing&f=magic-items) — Drink it to regain 2d4 + 2 hit points.
[**Potion of Climbing**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=potion+of+climbing&f=magic-items) — Gain a climbing speed for 1 hour, with advantage not to slip.
[**Potion of Water Breathing**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=potion+of+water+breathing&f=magic-items) — Breathe underwater for 1 hour.
[**Spell Scroll (1st Level)**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=spell+scroll&f=magic-items) — Cast one inscribed 1st-level spell with no slot or components.
[**Driftglobe**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=driftglobe&f=magic-items) — A glass sphere that floats and sheds light or daylight on command.
[**Moon-Touched Sword**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=moon-touched+sword&f=magic-items) — A blade that sheds moonlight in a 15-foot radius.
[**Cloak of Billowing**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=cloak+of+billowing&f=magic-items) — Billows dramatically on command — pure theatrics.
[**Hat of Wizardry**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=hat+of+wizardry&f=magic-items) — Cast a wizard cantrip, and once a day attempt an unprepared spell.
[**Wand of Magic Detection**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=wand+of+magic+detection&f=magic-items) — Casts detect magic three times per day.
[**Wand of Secrets**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=wand+of+secrets&f=magic-items) — Points toward a hidden door or trap within 30 feet, twice a day.
[**Rope of Mending**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=rope+of+mending&f=magic-items) — A cut piece of this rope fuses back into one length.
[**Cloak of Many Fashions**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=cloak+of+many+fashions&f=magic-items) — Change its colour, cut, and condition at will.
[**Boots of False Tracks**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=boots+of+false+tracks&f=magic-items) — Leave the footprints of another humanoid species.
[**Charlatan's Die**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=charlatan%27s+die&f=magic-items) — A die you can magically force to any result.

magic.body:
Magic Treasure
==============

Magic items, grouped by rarity tier. The tool weights which **tier** is rolled
by party level (and slightly by party size); it then picks a random item from
that tier. To roll by hand, choose a tier and roll on it (Common d16, Uncommon
d16, Rare d8). Tiers map to standard 5e rarities; every item name links to its
D&D Beyond page.

## Common

1. [**Potion of Healing**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=potion+of+healing&f=magic-items) — Drink it to regain 2d4 + 2 hit points.
2. [**Potion of Climbing**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=potion+of+climbing&f=magic-items) — Gain a climbing speed for 1 hour, with advantage not to slip.
3. [**Potion of Water Breathing**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=potion+of+water+breathing&f=magic-items) — Breathe underwater for 1 hour.
4. [**Spell Scroll (1st Level)**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=spell+scroll&f=magic-items) — Cast one inscribed 1st-level spell with no slot or components.
5. [**Driftglobe**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=driftglobe&f=magic-items) — A glass sphere that floats and sheds light or daylight on command.
6. [**Moon-Touched Sword**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=moon-touched+sword&f=magic-items) — A blade that sheds moonlight in a 15-foot radius.

[**Winged Boots**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=winged+boots&f=magic-items) — Sprout wings for a flying speed for up to 4 hours a day.

[**Wings of Flying**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=wings+of+flying&f=magic-items) — A cloak that becomes wings for a 60-foot fly speed for 1 hour.
[**Horn of Blasting**](https://www.dndbeyond.com/search?q=horn+of+blasting&f=magic-items) — Sound it for a damaging, deafening thunderclap in a cone.

Discoveries: **Ancient druid grove blessing** — The old powers of the wood grant a lasting boon to those who tend it.
**Friendly dragon contact** — A reclusive dragon takes an interest, offering counsel or a future favour.
**Fey crossing** — A shimmering boundary where the Feywild bleeds through; a shortcut, or a doorway.
**Lost shrine granting a boon** — A forgotten altar that rewards the reverent with a one-time blessing.
**Permanent safe campsite** — A hidden, defensible hollow the party can always return to for safe rest.
**Secret shortcut** — A hidden pass or game trail that removes several days of travel.
**Map to a forgotten dungeon** — Directions to an unlooted ruin, sealed since before living memory.
**A bound elemental owing one service** — A spirit waiting to be freed in exchange for a single task.
**An ancient treant** — A slow, watchful giant willing to share a long-kept secret of the wood.
**A star-metal deposit** — Raw sky-iron, enough to forge one special item.
**A sentient magic weapon** — A blade with its own voice, will, and agenda, seeking a worthy wielder.
**A hidden portal to a demiplane refuge** — A keyed doorway to a safe pocket realm of rest.
**A celestial messenger** — A winged emissary bearing a quest and a blessing.
**A long-dead archmage's cache** — A sealed vault holding one scroll of 3rd level or higher.

wondrous.body:
Wondrous Discovery
==================

The natural-20 result. The find is often watched over by a **guardian**. The tool
randomizes this with equal odds: nothing, a high-severity environmental hazard, or
a boss-tier monster scaled to the party (drawn from `tools/encounter.py`'s data;
see `tools/loot.py`).

## Treasures

links to its D&D Beyond page.

Instead of pure treasure, make it a story **discovery**. These tend to create
more memorable moments than simply handing out a powerful item, so they carry no
item link.
